title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
Account Number Routing (ANR)
Account Number Routing is the ability to send a payment through
Interac
e-Transfer
脗庐
by providing the banking account details (Institution Number, Transit Number, and Account Number) of the recipient. Account Number Routing is a feature introduced with
Interac
e-Transfer for Business.
Autodeposit
Autodeposit is a feature that allows transfers through
Interac
e-Transfer to be deposited directly into customer accounts without the need to log in to online banking to accept the funds. A customer must register an email address with
Interac
脗庐
and associate it with their online bank account to use Autodeposit.
Autodeposit Registration
Autodeposit Registration is the registration with
Interac
e-Transfer of a customer profile containing an email address and an online bank account, used for Autodeposit.
Blocked / Not Blocked
Not blocked is the default state for an
Interac
e-Transfer transaction. A transaction can be moved to a blocked state by Central 1 or by
Interac
if it is deemed to be fraudulent or suspicious.
Blocked List
A blocked list is a list of email addresses, or a list of mobile phone numbers, deemed fraudulent or suspicious by
Interac,
Central 1 FAS, or a financial institution OAS Fraud Analyst. Any
Interac
e-Transfer transaction associated with a recipient芒聙聶s email address or mobile phone number currently on a blocked list is subject to an
Interac
fraud analysis review and may be blocked and reported by
Interac
as fraud-suspicious. Blocked lists are only accessible to
Interac
. Any
Interac
e-Transfer customer profile associated with an email or a mobile phone number currently on a blocked list may be disabled by Central 1 FAS or a financial institution OAS Fraud Analyst. Blocked email addresses or mobile phone numbers will be automatically removed by
Interac
from a blocklist periodically if they are not related to any new fraudulent activity. See Disabled/Enabled,
Interac
e-Transfer Profile, and OAS Fraud Analyst.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1芒聙聶s applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users can芒聙聶t manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
Contact
The contact is the person (known by either an email address, a mobile phone number, or a banking account) to whom funds are sent through an
Interac
e-Transfer transaction. See Account Number Routing (ANR).
Disabled/Enabled
Disabled is a temporary state of a customer芒聙聶s
Interac
e-Transfer profile, as set by Central 1
or a financial institution OAS Fraud Analyst
.
By default, an
Interac
e-Transfer profile is in the enabled state, allowing the customer to send or receive transfers through
Interac
e-Transfer. Profiles associated with transactions that are deemed fraudulent or suspicious, or associated with emails or mobile phone numbers currently on a blocked list, may be disabled by Central 1 or a financial institution OAS Fraud Analyst. Customers with disabled profiles can芒聙聶t send or receive transfers through
Interac
e-Transfer. See
Interac
e-Transfer Profile and OAS Fraud Analyst.
Expired
Expired is the status assigned by
Interac
to an
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction that wasn芒聙聶t deposited or cancelled within 30 days. An expired Send Money transaction can芒聙聶t be deposited and must be cancelled by the sender. Send Money
transactions that are expired for more than 30 days are cancelled by
Interac
as part of the Lazy Sender process.
Expired is also the status applied to an
Interac
e-Transfer Request Money transaction that wasn芒聙聶t fulfilled by the request recipient within 30 days of notification (or re-notification). An expired Request Money transaction can芒聙聶t be fulfilled. No settlement is required for expired requests since funds aren't moved when requests are sent.
Failed
Failed is a reporting designation used by Central 1 to refer to
Interac
e-Transfer transactions that aren't Completed or Cancelled, due to one of the following:
A technical error.
An
Interac
e-Transfer transaction has been blocked or the recipient芒聙聶s email or mobile phone number is on a blocked list.
The sender芒聙聶s outgoing
Interac
e-Transfer limit has been reached.
Failed transactions are those that appear on the ECCX (e-Transfer Failed Transactions Report). See聽the
Interac
e-Transfer Settlement, Tracing, and Reports Part for more information on
Interac
e芒聙聭Transfer reconciliation reports, including the ECCX report.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download and upload files from and to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
FI Reference Number
See Retrieval Reference Number. FI Reference Number is the name of a search option found in the drop-down menu when performing searches in OAS. See Section 4.2, Basic Searches in OAS. The FI Reference Number corresponds to the unique reference number assigned by Central 1 to
Interac
e-Transfer transactions, which can be found on the
Interac
e-Transfer reconciliation reports. See the
Interac
e-Transfer Settlement, Tracing, and Reports Part for more information on
Interac
e-Transfer reconciliation reports.
Fulfill Request
Fulfilling a Request for Money refers to a customer initiating a Send Money transfer by accepting a Request for Money. Customers fulfilling requests can review the request details and agree to transfer funds to the sender of the request. They can also decline the request or prevent future requests from being received from this sender or from any sender. In OAS, transactions that fulfill a Request for Money are cross-referenced to the request they fulfilled in the Transfer Details of the Send Money transaction that fulfills the request and in the Request Details of the request itself.
Funds Recovery Request
A request sent through OAS between the sending and receiving financial institutions for the return of funds from a fraudulent
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction. The request appears on the Funds Recovery tab of the Transfer Details page of the transfer. Only OAS Fraud Analysts can initiate, respond to, or view these requests.
Handle
A 芒聙聹handle芒聙聺 is another term
Interac
uses to describe the notification channels (email addresses, mobile phone numbers, or banking account details) through which Send Money and Request Money transfers can be sent.
Interac
e-Transfer for Business
A collection of newer features added to
Interac
e-Transfer to support the commercial use of
Interac
e-Transfer for settling business transactions. These features included sending and viewing remittance details with a transfer, sending transfers directly to a recipient芒聙聶s bank account through Account Number Routing, and, for incoming transfers, the elimination of rolling transfers limits and the introduction of a maximum limit of $25,000 per transfer.
Interac
e-Transfer Profile
An
Interac
e-Transfer profile is record of a customer芒聙聶s contact details registered with
Interac.
The customer芒聙聶s profile is
associated with the customer芒聙聶s use of the
Interac
e-Transfer service. The customer must register for an
Interac
e-Transfer profile before sending or receiving transfers through the
Interac
e-Transfer service. A profile may be disabled by Central 1 or an OAS Fraud Analyst if there is any suspicious activity.
Interac
Payment Reference
See
Interac
Reference Number. The
Interac
Payment Reference is the name of a search option found in the drop-down menu when performing basic searches in OAS. This number corresponds to the unique reference number that
Interac
assigns to
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transactions. See Section 4.2, Basic Searches in OAS.
Interac
Reference Number
The
Interac
Reference Number is a unique identifier that
Interac
assigns to each
Interac
e-Transfer transaction. It is also referred to in the search options as an
Interac
Payment Reference (for Send Money transactions) and
Interac
Request Reference (for Request settlement transactions), as found in the drop-down menu when performing basic searches in OAS. See Section 4.2, Basic Searches in OAS.
Interac
Request Reference
See
Interac
Reference Number. The
Interac
Request Reference is the name of a search option found in the drop-down menu when performing basic searches in OAS. This number corresponds to the unique reference number that
Interac
assigns to
Interac
e-Transfer Request Money transactions. See Section 4.2, Basic Searches in OAS.
Interrupted Transfers
An Interrupted Transfer is a transfer that has been left in an inconsistent state due to a breakdown or timeout during communications with your banking host or with
Interac,
or due to the transfer being abandoned or cancelled by the customer. No action is required by your financial institution regarding the interrupted transfers appearing in OAS, as both
Interac
and/or Central 1 have processes in place to correct these transactions.
Lazy Sender Process
The Lazy Sender process is an
Interac
e-Transfer process where Send Money transactions set to status Expired for 30 days are cancelled by
Interac
. If a transaction hasn芒聙聶t been cancelled or received for a total of 60 days (30 days at status Requested and 30 days at status Expired),
Interac
cancels the transaction within seven days. Central聽1 then refunds the funds back to the senders芒聙聶 accounts.
Limit Group
A limit group defines the maximum amount of funds that customers who are assigned to it can send through
Interac
e-Transfer.
Note:	Previous limits on receiving incoming transfers that were set through limit groups have been removed:
Interac
revoked daily, weekly, or monthly rolling limits on incoming transfers and replaced them with a single per transfer limit of $25,000.
OAS User Administrator
An OAS User Administrator is an individual at your financial institution who logs in to OAS to add or modify OAS Users.
OAS CSR (Customer Service Representative)
An OAS CSR is an individual at your financial institution who logs in to OAS with read-only access for handling customer inquiries regarding
Interac
e-Transfer transactions.
OAS Fraud Analyst
An OAS Fraud Analyst is an individual at your financial institution who logs in to OAS to investigate and action, where necessary, any potentially fraudulent
Interac
e-Transfer transactions.
OAS User ID
OAS User ID refers to the username used to log in to OAS.
Recipient
The recipient is the customer who is receiving funds through an
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction, identified through their email address, mobile phone number, or banking account details.
Retrieval Reference Number
The Retrieval Reference Number is a number generated by Central 1 that is unique to each
Interac
e芒聙聭Transfer transaction. Also referred to as the FI Reference Number in the drop-down menu displayed when performing searches in OAS. See Section 4.2, Basic Searches in OAS.
Request Money
Request Money is feature that allows the request sender to request funds from the request recipient through online banking. The recipient of the request receives an email notification from
Interac
and can either fulfill or decline the request. If the recipient fulfills the request, the request recipient芒聙聶s account is debited, and funds are deposited directly into the request sender芒聙聶s account.
Requested
Requested is the status assigned to an
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction that hasn芒聙聶t been received or cancelled. The transaction can remain at status 芒聙聹Requested芒聙聺 for up to 30 days. After 30 days, the status of the transaction changes to Expired.
Revoke
Revoke is a feature of OAS that allows an OAS CSR to remove an email address from the scrubbed email list. See Scrubbed Email Address and Scrubbed Email List.
An OAS CSR uses Revoke when they know the conditions causing undeliverable emails being bounced back to
Interac,
have now been, or are now being, corrected.
Rolling Limit
A rolling limit is the maximum total dollar amount of
Interac
e-Transfer transactions that a customer can send within a rolling time period.
Previous rolling limits on incoming transfers have been removed:
Interac
has revoked daily, weekly, or monthly rolling limits on incoming transfers, and has raised the limit on individual incoming transfers to $25,000 per transfer
.
Rolling Time Period
A rolling time period is a period between the moment of sending an
Interac
e-Transfer transaction (the current moment) and an earlier moment in time, calculated backwards, by a pre芒聙聭defined number of hours or days, to the minute from the current moment. The total amounts of a customer芒聙聶s completed
Interac
e-Transfer send transactions occurring in this period determines how much of the rolling limit has been used up and whether any room exists under the limit to allow the current
Interac
e-Transfer transaction to go forward. Current definitions of rolling time periods include 24-hour, 7-day, and 30-day periods.
Rolling Limit for a Rolling Time Period Example:
Assume that a $3,000 rolling limit for a 24-hour rolling time period for
Interac
Send Money transactions applies to a financial institution芒聙聶s customers. Assume also that yesterday a customer had
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transactions as follows:
$1,000.00 at 11:00 AM
$1,000.00 at 01:00 PM
$1,000.00 at 05:00 PM
The limit on Send Money transactions that the customer could send at sample selected times today, based on the rolling time period in effect at each time, and the amount of
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transactions completed in that period, would be as follows:
Time
Rolling Time Period in Effect
Rolling Limit Used
Remaining Limit
9:00 AM
Yesterday 9:00 AM 芒聙聯 Today 9:00 AM
$3,000
$0
12:00 AM
Yesterday 12:00 AM 芒聙聯 Today 12:00 AM
$2,000
$1,000
02:00 PM
Yesterday 02:00 PM 芒聙聯 Today 02:00 PM
$1,000
$2,000
06:00 PM
Yesterday 06:00 PM 芒聙聯 Today 06:00 PM
$0
$3,000
Previous rolling limits on incoming transfers have been removed:
Interac
has revoked daily, weekly, or monthly rolling limits on incoming transfers, and has raised the limit on individual incoming transfers to $25,000 per transfer
.
Scrubbed Email Address
A scrubbed email address is an email address, belonging to the sender or the recipient of an
Interac
e-Transfer transaction, that
Interac
has placed on the scrubbed email list because
Interac
email notifications sent to that address have been bounced back to
Interac芒聙聶s
email servers as undeliverable.
A scrubbed email address doesn't receive
Interac
e-Transfer transaction notifications.
An OAS CSR can revoke an email address from the scrubbed email list when the conditions causing emails sent to bounce back to
Interac
as undeliverable have now been, or are now being, corrected. See Revoke.
Scrubbed Email List
The scrubbed email list is the list of scrubbed email addresses maintained by
Interac.
Interac
e-Transfer transactions aren't sent to any email address on the scrubbed email list.
While there may be many reasons why an undelivered email can be bounced back to
Interac
, an email address is placed on the scrubbed email list if an email addressed to it is bounced back for any of the following, specific reasons:
Bad destination mailbox address
Unable to route
Address doesn't exist
Bad destination system address
An OAS CSR can revoke an email address from the scrubbed email list when they know that the conditions causing emails sent to that address to be bounced back to
Interac
as undeliverable have now been, or are now being, corrected. See Revoke.
Security Question
The security question is a question that the recipient of an
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction is asked, and must answer, to authenticate the transfer. The transaction sender creates the security question.
Note:	If a recipient attempts to complete the deposit of a Send Money transfer during a login to their online banking occurring after the login during which they answered the security question, the later login must be under the same membership as the previous login, or the deposit fails.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
Troubleshooting Scenarios
Question
A member receives an error when accepting an
Interac
e-Transfer money transfer and they don芒聙聶t see a credit in their banking account. What do we do?
Answer
1.
Locate the member in OAS by searching by Customer Email. See Figure 1.
2.
Find the transfer in the Received e-Transfer(s) section of the Customer Details screen. See Figure 2.
3.
Open the Transfer Details screen by selecting the transfer芒聙聶s
Interac
Payment Reference #. See Figure 2.
4.
Check the Status and Blocked fields under Transfer Details. See Figure 3.
5.
If transfer status is 芒聙聹
Pending
芒聙聺 and the transfer isn芒聙聶t blocked, have the customer retry accepting the deposit from the original
Interac
email/text message notification. If the customer has already answered the security question in their previous attempt to accept the transfer, they should accept the transfer into the same account that they had previously selected.
6.
If the transfer status is 芒聙聹
Cancelled
芒聙聺, advise the customer.
7.
If the transfer has been blocked, determine if it is a legitimate transaction. For legitimate transfers, have a CPS Admin Security Officer contact FAS to unblock the transfer, or unblock it yourself if your institution has a user with the OAS Fraud Analyst role.
Question
What do we do when a member sent an e-Transfer without any errors, but the recipient states they never received an email from
Interac
to pick up the money, and the money has been withdrawn from the member芒聙聶s account?
Answer
1.
Locate the member in OAS by searching by Customer Email. See Figure 1.
2.
Look for the transfer in the Sent e-Transfer(s) section under Customer Details. See Figure 4.
3.
If the transfer is found in the Sent e-Transfer(s) transfers list, open the Transfer Details by selecting the
Interac
Payment Reference #.
4.
Check the Status and Blocked fields under the Transfer Details. See Figure 3.
5.
If the transfer is blocked, determine if the transfer is a legitimate transaction. For legitimate transfers, have a CPS Admin Security Officer contact FAS to unblock the transfer, or unblock it yourself if your institution has a user with the OAS Fraud Analyst role.
6.
If the transfer isn芒聙聶t blocked and the transfer status is 芒聙聹
Pending
芒聙聺, have the member check with the recipient whether the recipient芒聙聶s email address is incorrect and whether
Interac
email notifications are being flagged as junk mail or spam.
7.
If the transfer can芒聙聶t be found under Sent e-Transfer(s), wait until the next day to verify the transfer appears as a failed transfer on the ECCX report, then credit the funds back to the member. The member may retry sending the transfer.
Question
What do we do when a member receives an error message when trying to send an
Interac
e-Transfer transfer and the funds remain in the member芒聙聶s account?
Answer
1.
Locate the member in OAS by searching by Customer Email.
2.
Check the Customer Details screen under the Profile Summary tab to see if the customer芒聙聶s profile has been disabled because of suspicious activity. See Figure 5.
3.
If the customer profile is enabled, have the customer retry sending the transfer. If the error persists, contact Digital Banking Support to investigate message logs for timeouts or communication errors.
Question
What do we do when a member accepts a transfer via
Interac
e-Transfer and receives no errors but doesn't see the credit in their account? The sender says they received an
Interac
notification saying it was completed.
Answer
1.
Locate the member in OAS by searching by Customer Email. See Figure 1.
2.
Find the transfer in the Received e-Transfer(s) section of the Customer Details screen. See Figure 2.
3.
Confirm that the
Status
column, as it appears against the transfer, shows that the transfer芒聙聶s status is 芒聙聹
Completed
芒聙聺.
4.
If the transfer status is 芒聙聹
Completed
芒聙聺 and the transfer doesn't show in the banking system, confirm the next day that the transaction appears on the ECCX failed transfers report and credit the member manually.
Question
What are the most common errors that cause transfers to fail?
Answer
1.
A recipient doesn芒聙聶t enter the answer to the security question exactly as it was spelled.
2.
A sender has insufficient funds in their account to complete the transfer.
3.
A recipient answers the security question using one login and tries to complete the transfer using another.
4.
A banking host times out when debiting or crediting a member芒聙聶s account and the transaction gets reversed.
5.
A recipient芒聙聶s email address is misspelled in the sender芒聙聶s recipient profile.
6.
Interac
email notifications are being intercepted as junk or spam emails.
7.
An
Interac
customer profile has been disabled or a transfer has been blocked.
Question
What happens when a member answers a question incorrectly three times when trying to pick up a transfer?
Answer
If a challenge question is answered incorrectly three times, the transfer is locked and can not be picked up online. The sender of the transaction must log in to online banking and cancel the transfer. A new transfer will have to be sent to the recipient.
Question
Why is it that when the status of a transfer is 芒聙聹E-Transfer Initiated芒聙聺, no notifications are sent?
Answer
When a transfer is created,
Interac
takes up to 30 minutes to do their security/fraud check and then sends out the notification to the recipient (this varies depending on your Limit Group settings). The status then changes to 芒聙聹
Notification sent
芒聙聺.
Question
What do we do when a member sends an
Interac
e-Transfer transaction to a recipient芒聙聶s mobile phone number but receives an email and/or text message saying it didn芒聙聶t go through?
Answer
1.
Locate the member in OAS by searching by Customer Email. See Figure 1.
2.
Find the transfer in the Sent e-Transfer(s) section under Customer Details. See Figure 4.
3.
Confirm that the status of the transfer, as it appears against the transfer in the
Status
column, reads 芒聙聹
Error notifying contact
芒聙聺.
4.
Advise the member that the mobile phone number they have recorded for the recipient wasn芒聙聶t contactable.
Note
: A mobile phone might not be contactable for a variety of reasons:
The phone number is for a landline.
The phone number is for a non-Canadian mobile phone.
The recipient has blocked
Interac
e-Transfer notifications from being sent to their mobile phone.
The recipient is experiencing technical issues with their phone or mobile network.
The mobile network operator doesn't support all text messages.
Question
A recipient has blocked
Interac
e-Transfer notifications to their mobile phone. What can we do?
Note: To permanently stop all text notifications from
Interac
e-Transfer, reply to an
Interac
e-Transfer text with STOP or text STOP to 100001.
Answer
Option 1
: The member can update recipient profile they created for this recipient with an email address and change the notification preference to 芒聙聹email芒聙聺.
Option 2
: The member can request that the recipient unblock their mobile phone number by texting START or RESTART to 100001.
Note
: Standard messaging rates apply to
Interac
text messages.
Question
What do we when a member is getting a limit error when sending a transfer?
Answer
1.
Locate the member in OAS by searching by Customer Email. See Figure 1.
2.
Review the member芒聙聶s outgoing e-Transfer limits and current usage on the Profile Summary page to determine which limit the transfer is exceeding (Per Transaction Limit, Daily Rolling Limit, 7-Day Rolling Limit, or 30-Day Rolling Limit). See Figure 6.
3.
To calculate when they can send the transfer again, count back from the current date and time by 24 hours, 7 days, or 30 days (depending on which limit is exceeded) to determine, to the exact minute, the earliest date/time that a transfer must be dated to be included in the rolling time period. The oldest transfers, dated between that date and now, determine when the calculated usage will change and by how much. When older transfers fall out of the rolling period, they will drop out of the calculations of limit usage, freeing up room under the limit for additional transfers to be created.
Note
:
Interac
doesn't choose the limits for a financial institution, nor does Central 1. This is a decision made by the institution from limit groups made available by Central 1. If you wish customized limits, it would be a billable project to enable all your users (Retail or Small Business).
Question
A member has more than 1000 Received transfers, and we get a warning message when we try to view the transfers from the Customer Details screen. The transfers either don芒聙聶t display or display eventually after an extremely long wait time. What should we do?
Answer
1.
Copy the member芒聙聶s email address or client ID from the Customer Details screen.
2.
Select
Advanced Search
(the magnifying glass icon).
3.
Select
Transfer
as the Advanced Search category. See Figure 7.
4.
Set the first filter to either filter type
Payer Customer ID
(if you copied the member芒聙聶s client ID) or
Contact Email
(if you copied the member芒聙聶s email address) and paste what you copied into the accompanying text box. See Figure 7.
5.
Add a second filter by selecting the plus sign icon aligned with the first filter.
6.
For the second filter, select the filter type
Date Sent
. See Figure 7.
7.
Select
Date Range
from the accompanying pop-up options menu, then use the
Start date
and
End date
calendar controls to set a narrow transactions date range.
8.
Close the pop-up, then select
Search
. A search is performed
and
only transfers falling within the specified date range display on a Payments screen.
Note:
OAS limits the number of transfers it displays to 1000. Observe if the Payments screen displays a brief message indicating that the number of transfers from the search exceeds 1000. Also check the summary description of transfers being displayed (example, 芒聙聹
Showing 1芒聙聯15 of 1000
芒聙聺). Narrow the date range further if needed to ensure the search results contain fewer than 1000 transfers.
Note
: If you are searching for Sent transfers where more than 1000 transfers exist for a member, you need to copy the member芒聙聶s client ID and use the filter type
Payer Customer ID
as your first filter. Unlike Received transfers, Sent transfers can芒聙聶t be searched by the sender芒聙聶s email address using the Advanced Search transfer category filter types.
Figure 1: Searching OAS by Customer Email
Figure 2: Received e-Transfer(s)
Figure 3: Transfer Details 芒聙聯 Blocked and Status Fields
Figure 4: Sent e-Transfer(s)
Figure 5: Disabled Customer Profile
Figure 6: Customer Transfer Limits
Figure 7: Advanced Search 芒聙聯 Transfers
OAS Fraud Analyst Role
Question
Is there a cost to implement the OAS Fraud Analyst role?
Answer
No, there is no cost. Implementing the OAS Fraud Analyst role is not mandatory. However, financial institutions with the OAS Fraud Analyst role can greatly enhance their control over a customer芒聙聶s
Interac
e-Transfer functionality. They can disable and enable a customer芒聙聶s profile due to concerns such as misuse and AML issues. Financial institutions can also submit funds recovery requests faster since they don芒聙聶t have to go through Central 1. Funds recovery is time-sensitive, and the earlier the request is made, the higher the chances of recovering funds.
Question
Our agreement with Central 1 to implement the OAS Fraud Analyst role states that failure to use our Central 1 fraud management system (FAS or EFM) to update and log fraud alerts incurs a fee if Central 1 updates and logs the alert on our behalf. Is there a grace period?
Answer
Charging a fee would be Central 1芒聙聶s last resort after all other communications have been exhausted and no corrective actions have been taken. Generally, we would remind the financial institution when we identify situations where fraudulent payments have had fund recovery requests made through OAS, but no fraud alert was created through EFM (for clients on EFM) or requested to be created by email to Central 1 (for clients on FAS). If no actions are taken after multiple communication attempts, then Central 1 will bill the financial institution.
Question
How long do we need to adopt the new funds recovery process after getting the OAS Fraud Analyst role?
Answer
Our FAS team can support your current funds recovery process for up to 6 months after your team receives the OAS Fraud Analyst role. Generally, from initial pilot launch, we noticed most financial institutions choose anywhere between 1 to 4 weeks to take over the streamlined funds recovery process.
Question
Will automatic email updates be delivered to the same email group that the EFM/FAS alerts go to, or will they only be delivered to the OAS Fraud Analyst that submitted the request for funds recovery or updated a customer profile in OAS?
Answer
We highly recommend forwarding
Interac
email notifications to a team inbox to prevent disrupting or delaying any pending funds recovery process. However, we can forward the notifications to a few specific individual emails if needed.
END
